message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_diff preserves leading dims and rejects rank-0 (frankentorch-rjn4)

tensor_diff previously collapsed the output to shape [0] whenever the
input's last dim was < 2. PyTorch parity requires that only the last
dim shrink to 0 — e.g. diff(zeros(3, 1)) -> shape [3, 0], not [0].
Rank-0 input was also silently accepted and produced shape [0]; PyTorch
raises a RuntimeError ("diff expects input to be at least
one-dimensional").

Fix in both F64 and F32 branches:
- shape.is_empty() -> propagate AutogradError::Dispatch with the same
  diagnostic the existing else-branch uses.
- last_dim < 2 -> build the output shape by cloning the input shape and
  setting its last entry to 0; allocate an empty values vector with
  that shape.

Tests:
- diff_preserves_leading_dims_when_last_dim_lt_2 (F64)
- diff_preserves_leading_dims_when_last_dim_lt_2_f32 (F32)
- diff_rejects_rank_zero_input (F64)
- diff_rejects_rank_zero_input_f32 (F32)

All 1086 ft-api tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
